title: Sour Flaky Dutch Apple Crumble
description: A comforting and nostalgic Dutch apple crumble featuring a blend of sweet applesauce with a tangy glaze and flaky crust, perfect for breakfast or dessert.

ingredients.name: 3/4 cup unsalted butter, cold and cubed
3 cups peeled and chopped apples (about 6 medium)

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). In a mixing bowl, combine flour and salt. Add in cold cubed butter using the 'peel-and-grate' method until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
In another bowl, mix sugar and lemon juice; add chopped apples and stir gently to coat with sugar-lemon mixture.
Pour apple filling into an 8x8 inch baking dish lined with parchment paper. Sprinkle half of flour-crust mixture evenly over the top of apples.
Bake for about 35-40 minutes or until apples are tender and crust is golden brown. Allow to cool slightly before serving. Top with remaining crumb mixture if desired.
Serve warm, optionally drizzled with extra glaze made from lemon juice and sugar.
